text.title: security-container-scanning Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for security-container-scanning. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every image build in CI MUST run `trivy image` (or equivalent) and MUST exit non-zero on CRITICAL or HIGH vulnerabilities before the image is pushed to any registry.
rule.rationale: Pushing a vulnerable image to a registry makes it available for deployment; blocking at build time prevents vulnerable images from ever entering the artifact store.

rule.statement: Generate a CycloneDX SBOM per build and store as an immutable artifact attached to the release. Do not mix CycloneDX and SPDX formats in the same pipeline — pick one and stick to it.
rule.rationale: SBOM format mixing breaks downstream consumers (Dependency-Track, SIEM integrations) that are format-specific. Immutable storage ensures the SBOM matches the exact image used in production.

rule.statement: Use Sigstore cosign keyless OIDC signing in CI. Never commit cosign private keys to the repository — even in secrets.
rule.rationale: Keyless signing uses the CI provider's OIDC identity token to attest the image via Rekor's transparency log; there is no key to rotate, leak, or lose. Committing keys to repos (even encrypted) creates a permanent audit trail of mishandled secrets.
